persons.arguments_against: The strongest counter-hypothesis is a same-name relative (e.g. a son) holding a different Bermuda post in the same years, or that the staff listing reflects an honoured retiree rather than current service. Both fail: the corpus contains exactly one Bols official and one Bols biography; the stint records the full distinctive forename pair 'Louis Jean' with the rank Lieut.-General and the precise office 'Governor and Commander-in-Chief', matching the biography's 'gov. and commdr. in ch., Bermuda, 1927' appointment which was current (appointed 1927, listed 1928-1930); and three independent honours (K.C.B., K.C.M.G., D.S.O.) match exactly. A second 'Sir Louis Jean Bols, K.C.B., K.C.M.G., D.S.O., Lieut.-General' governing Bermuda at the same time is not a realistic hypothesis.

evidence_cited.fact: gov. and commdr. in ch., Bermuda, 1927

persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second contemporaneous Louis Jean Bols (e.g. a son with identical forenames and honours), or a printed record showing the biography subject died or left office before 1928 while the staff lists continued under a different person.

notes: Single-bio, single-stint case with full forename, exact office, exact place, contiguous dates (appointment 1927, listings 1928-1930), three shared honours, and matching rank. No live counter-hypothesis.
